reviews.review_description: It. Feels so real. I’ve loved pinball since I was a teen. I got the Charlie Brown table first then I got the one with Night Rider in it. Started is next. I’m also looking at the Kong tables. Great job! 😊 thank you. Would you create a pinball game in honor of Michael Jackson? I’d appreciate it.

reviews.review_title: Diner pack doesn't appear in app
reviews.review_description: Just purchased the Diner 3 pack and it isn't in the game anywhere, even after the update. WTF. Also, you really need to optimize this app to load faster and improve the resolution. I have a Quest 3 and it looks like it was built for Quest 1. Please fix the issue with the new table pack, I paid $22 CAD and the tables aren't available to me.

developer_response.body:
Hi, thank you for the feedback. The Williams Pinball Volume 10 tables (Diner, Fire!, Comet) do not have a dedicated room. Due to performance considerations, adding more rooms and cabinets can negatively impact performance, so we need to stay within certain technical limits. Instead, the new tables can be placed in the cabinet slots in any of the existing Williams rooms or in the favorite table slot. Thank you for your understanding!

Also, we're sorry to hear about the loading time! Does it load for a long time at every startup, or did it only happen for the first time after the patch?

reviews.review_description:
NOTE:  AFTER THE LAST UPDATE Not sure if it was yours or the V81 for Meta I lost all my collectables and things I won playing the games, statues, posters, shelf decorations to all my games.  It still shows my high scores (one of them I was #6 in the world) and I still have all the trophies on the back shelving and turning wheel, but everything else was lost.  I had maxed out Indiana Jones and was close to maxing out (Level 5) on Addams Family and Star Trek.  Can you help me regain all those items back?    I also had 80+ out of the 480+ stars at the globe with the world contests.  Thank you for your attention to this. 
UPDATE:  Yes, I have tried to contact them TWICE about two weeks apart and after three weeks still have not heard back from them. I even gave them links to my VR Game review page where I have videos of me walking through the arcade showing the statues, posters and shelf items that were there and now they are all gone. They just don't seem to care at all.
UPDATE TO THE UPDATE:  It has been several months now since I contacted your customer service about losing everything, I worked so hard on to earn playing your games when the V81 for Meta happened and STILL NO RESPONSE.  I LOVE your games but HATE your customer service. 
UPDATE 2/1/26 I have attempted several times since Oct. 2025 to get your customer service to restore everything I lost after the update and STILL HAVE NOT HEARD A SINGLE WORD. I started you at a five star and continue to drop your rating until this is fixed.
UPDATE 6/21/26 - It's been nine months and still not a word about solving this problem or any fixes done. I went from rating this game a five to a one, not because of the games, but because of the horrible customer service.  I can't go any lower for this app, so I'll guess I'll have to start lowering the other pinball apps I have for Zen Studios until this is resolved.

I was already familiar with Zen Studio's previous VR pinball game and I loved it but this one takes it to the next level. It is very immersive having a whole arcade filled with pinball machines all for myself, being able to explore and customize it. The place is brimming with atmosphere. If you see this, Zen Studio, I would love to see you bring your Jurassic Park and Jurassic World tables to this game! I have been hoping to experience them in VR for a long time, and dinosaur statues would be fantastic decorations for my new arcade room ;)

developer_response.body: Hi, thanks for your nice words, we highly appreciate it! It would be cool to bring the Jurassic World Pinball tables to Pinball FX VR, hopefully, we can make it happen in the future. Wishing you a wonderful game!

reviews.review_description:
This is the way to play pinball… I’m so addicted to this game it’s not even funny. The only complaint I have is I wish the devs would integrate the old games tables (Universal Jaws/back to the future/ET) + The Walking Dead tables. And even better, how bout integrating the Star Wars tables as well? Have all the tables in 1 game so I don’t have open different apps in order to play a certain table.  
That complaint aside, this game has everything I could ever ask for in a pinball game. The unlockables are a great addition.  Makes it so I wanna play every table so I can unlock all the balls and flippers.

reviews.review_title: VR pinball isn’t just a gimmick
reviews.review_description: I originally thought that pinball in VR was just a gimmick, the tables on the tv worked just fine. But I was wrong! Having the table in front of you like it actually would be makes a big difference. It really recreates the experience of playing pinball. Then when you add the number of tables, the different modes and many collectibles, this is a home run.

Pocketeer Billiards is place near me with over 100 pinball machines and I have played hundreds of hours of real pinball there over the past 4 or 5 years. This game is so close to real pinball that it is pretty hard to believe I am home sitting on the couch. The graphics and amazing, the physics of the actual pinball seem perfect. This game has about 17 (out of over 30) different pinball machines that I have played at the actual arcade and you just can't find any differences from the real arcade versions. Keep making new tables and I will keep purchasing them!

I use it a lot, and my only problem is after the rounds the menu always comes up asking if i would like to play another.(of course i do, that's why i am at the table) The menu floating around each round is taking out from the immersion, also for some tables it's floating before the random number slot is rolled, so you don't have that last piece of experience for full immersion. Other than these, it's a cool game!

reviews.review_title: Amazing and fantastic hmm amazingly fant
reviews.review_description:
You get the drift, ever since playing pinball fx, fx2, and fx3 on my Xbox's now transforming them to my meta pro headset in VR and augmented reality is so freaking awesome. Man this is going to cost me more as I have the classic bundle but that is not as good,  the value is all there. I like the feel of walking into the arcade a real cool feeling but the best is playing in my living room feels like the machine is right there and you can have multiple machines walking from one to the other when done your game.

Make sure you create a large boundary if you can do the whole room much better.

Only hicup is I can't tell what machines I paid for and have to go over the entire collection with no special marking to indicate purchased please have a tab with "my machines" or something, I dont mind playing the demos and will most likely buy them all anyways but for the time there are a good 5 pages and hard to find my tables.

The quest pro has a few glitchy patches on some of the animations, but pretty much clears up and game continues as normal

Thanks for this amazing game.

Great app, the pinball simulation and details are truly fantastic!  Bought all the DLCs, the replay values are high so imo it’s fairly priced.  Highly recommend for meta quest 3!! 

To Developer:  please update the MR mode so that the table Heights could be adjusted (maybe there is a MR seated mod I haven’t discovered yet), and get rid of the MR storage door or make it an option to turn that on or off in MR (it’s super buggy).  Basically would be great to see improvements for future updates in MR mode and hopefully add multiplayer mode.

reviews.review_title: Thousands of pounds, in my living room!
reviews.review_description: This game is amazing! Oh, wait a minute, in Mr., it's insane!!!! Pinball games in my living room! Amazing, the downside, to many choices, and yes, it does rack up a bill. Once you have them all, just purchase them as they come out. No more putting money in the arcade. I love it!

I unfortunately picked this up along with the Star Trek DLC. I know it has tables built in, but I don't care about those. I'm here for Star Trek.


So I buy this and the Star Trek DLC. When you're thrown into the main hub area, you are unable to sort or filter what tables you own or do not own. There's a map, but it doesn't tell you what you own or don't own. You have to wander around and figure out what you can and can't play. Some stuff is obvious, but a lot of it isn't. It becomes a billboard for demos. I have no idea what I can and can't play, but I know I'm being encouraged to spend money. I don't even know where to find the tables I own in the hub world. Just an awful experience, every step of the way is encouraging you to spend money. Predatory. Maybe the developers want to make a good game, but the publishers just want you to spend money.


You can't adjust the table height in mixed reality mode. That is the only mode I'm interested in playing. And did I mention you can't sort by what tables you own or don't own? So in MR mode, when you try to place a table, do you own it? Don't know until you try to play it. Then you have to navigate the awful UI to remove the table, grab another, place it, and hope it's one that came packaged with the game.


I wanted something casual I could sit down and play while keeping an eye on my pets. But given that rudimentary features are missing from the MR mode, I can't do that. I can't explore the content that came with the game, because I don't know what I can and can't play, because it doesn't tell you, because they want you to spend money. And I can't refund it, because you can't refund the DLC, so I'm just stuck with it. I can refund the main game, but not the DLC. Then I'd be out the cost of the DLC.


It's a poorly designed game, that runs poorly on a Quest 3S, and is designed to get the user to buy things on impulse.


It's a dishonest effort at bringing pinball to the masses.

developer_response.body:
Hello, sorry for not hitting the mark! We're always trying to make our games more enjoyable with new models and practices, and refining visual clarity to enhance the gameplay experience. We hope you'll have a better experience next time! 

At the moment, there isn’t an option to recalibrate height in MR. I forwarded this to the developers as a feature request, it would be nice to make it happen in the future. In the meantime, please try rescanning your room! Placing both controllers on the floor helps Quest set the floor height more accurately. The game uses that height as the base value. 

The tables that come with the base game are Pinball Noir, Curse of the Mummy, and Sky Pirates: Treasures of the Clouds, as listed on the Pinball FX VR store page.

 Thank you for your honest feedback, it'll help in the future!

reviews.review_title: My Favorite Game to Play On The Quest
reviews.review_description: I have slowly collected all the dlc. What an amazing game. It truly feels like I have a massive arcade to spend hours in. I have had zen tables on the console, on a digital pinball machine and PC but this is my absolute favorite way to play them. It runs pretty much perfectly. It's well optimized and looks great and I assume when the next generation of quest comes out that it will look and run even better. If you get the game pick up one of the dlc bundles too and just have a blast exploring the machines. I'm still finding new ones I love more than I thought I would originally.

I’m playing Pinball FX VR on Meta Quest 3 and I’m a big fan of your PB games, the game are awesome, but not having gamepad support in Pinball FX VR is super annoying. Playing with a gamepad felt way closer to the real arcade experience. In older Zen Studios games, gamepads worked perfectly and the gameplay felt much more legit, but here I’m stuck using just the Touch controllers. Can you guys please add BT gamepad support?

developer_response.body: Hi, thank you for your review! The product is optimized for use with the official Meta controllers and the Arcade2TV-XR controller. Other third-party solutions may function, but they are not tested during development, and we are unable to provide assistance with troubleshooting any issues that may arise when using them. Thanks for your understanding!

reviews.review_description: After many years of playing digital pinball (Zens tables/Farsight) VR is the best version of these tables and i never want to go back to the regular flat product. This is a real winner, bring on as many tables as possible.

I was addicted to this game at release, and they started out great! Over time though, new tables have performance issues, the arcade room is poorly designed/expanded and made performance worse, longstanding issues haven't been resolved. Their Discord server is a perfect example of false consensus, old pinheads who dominate the conversation and shutdown any opinions outside their own. Even their community manager just doesn't respond to people unless it's her protection guard.

developer_response.body: Hi, thanks for your comments. Sorry to hear about the performance issues. Could I ask on which tables you experience this? We are actively looking into ways to expand the VR arcade in the future. Hopefully, we will have more information on it soon! I'm Balti, the Community Manager. I try to reply to every bug report in the server and continuously monitor the chat. Unfortunately, I might not always be able to respond to every message in real time due to other responsibilities I have. Thanks for your understanding!

reviews.review_description: The tables and mechanics are top notch. Great when played on quest and even better when you combine it with the xarcade. The only downside that I have is that they don't have enough tables but they are still releasing more every few months. Playing on the xarcade sometimes your arms will go crazy. While that's a little annoying it doesn't affect the game play. Not sure if that's a quest problem or xarcade problem. Highly recommend if you like pinball

Brilliant game - one of the most replayable games and really immersive and nice arcade experience
( re - reviewed today and still an amazing game but am really disappointed that the latest DLC doesnt have a designated place in the virtual arcade as all previous DLCs have done - I sincerely hope the devs take this on board and that future DLCs will have their spit in the arcade especially as people are paying for these DLCs)

A mixed reality masterpiece.
The best tables in history, as if they are in the room with you.
I’ve been doing VR since day one, had multiple iterations of Vive headsets for Steam VR and both PSVR 1 & 2, for several reasons was never going to do anything Oculus/Meta, this is what changed that and brought me in.
They’ve added most of the tables I suggested, still need Circus Voltaire, Fun House, Hurricane and others but what would be so great, is if they could just find a way to finally bring in Stern, so all their modern tables could be added, and Jersey Jacks, because all of their tables have the most advanced lighting.
P.S. Please bring in all the Pinball FX M Tables, or make Pinball FX M for VR.
Thanks.

A new place just opened in my area, The Pinball Capital in Stone Park, Ill. 120 tables, $20 play all day. Go.

Update - May 1st

Williams Volume 10!

I have just purchased the latest tables and updated the app today but no pinball tables from volume 10 are available or showing in the main game (I’ve looked everywhere !)

I uninstalled the app and reinstalled it and it’s the same, it shows I’ve purchased the tables in the app but they are nowhere to be found.

Is this a glitch your end? Please advise ?

……..



I’ve purchased all the tables, but PLEASE can you recreate Sorceress Lair, it’s such an amazing table, PLEASE✌️🙏🏻🙏🏻🙏🏻





Please, please recreate Sorceress Lair for this series...🙏🙏🙏




UPDATE: 27th July 2025

Purchased every table, it's just simply amazing.

I wondered, please could you add 'Sorcerers Lair' pinball table? It's my all time favourite, is this possible? Can it be done? 

Many thanks.

purchased the vol 2 days ago  had previous demoed the diner table and when i received the game that was the only game that loaded  in inventory  shows diner but the other 2 tables shows in installed but  shadowed out in my meta 3s
thank you

developer_response.body: Hi! You might not be able to find the Williams Pinball Volume 10 tables as the new tables do not have a dedicated room in Pinball FX VR. Due to performance considerations, adding more rooms and cabinets can negatively impact performance, so we need to stay within certain technical limits. Instead, the new tables can be placed in the cabinet slots in any of the existing Williams rooms or in the favorite table slot. We are actively looking into ways to expand the VR arcade in the future, and we are sorry for the confusion! Thank you for your review.

reviews.review_title: It's the same games I played at arcades
reviews.review_description: If this doesn't bring back nostalgia I don't know what does and the fact that it's in VR is even better all the effects oh my God you're dumb enough to get it

Pinball FX VR is more than just the standard Pinball FX in VR. Along with the sense of depth and visual clarity that comes with VR is an opulent attention to detail. Menus are snappy and functional in ways the flat screen app cannot compete with. The campaign mode is fun, logical and rewards you with machine customisation, such as custom flippers and balls, which are absent in the non VR versions.

It runs great on Quest 2 (particularly when enhanced with QGO) and even better on Q3. 

6 Stars if we can get Vol. 4-8 of Williams tables in here :)

I absolutely love Pinball FX VR. Once you experience pinball in mixed reality, it’s hard to go back to a 2D screen. It just doesn’t compare. This really feels like the next best thing to playing on a real machine. The tables are fantastic, and it’s great to see so many licensed, real-world machines that you’d typically find in arcades.

I’m really excited to see how this app continues to develop, and I can’t wait for more tables to be released! That said, I’d love to see a feature added in the future:

Multiplayer: My wife and I often play side by side on separate devices, calling out what’s happening in our games. It would be amazing to actually see each other’s gameplay in real time, or to have a pass-and-play option for when friends are around. 

Overall, this is an incredible experience and easily one of the most immersive ways to play pinball outside of owning a real machine.

reviews.review_title: This is a killer app for VR!
reviews.review_description: After spending almost a month with this, I can say this is the first VR anything that makes me want to put on the helmet everyday.  I've purchased every table, and will buy anything they add in the future.  Any negative reviews about price are ignorant.  If you are even a casual pinball fan, you are getting tremendous value for what you pay.  If you are a pinball nut, this is a total no-brainer!  I cannot wait to see what the future holds for this!

I have to say these are the most awesome pinball games I've ever played you guys do an amazing job I mean freaking amazing the graphics on these and the love that you put into it with the background and animations and all that are just freaking amazing I am blown away I love love love these pinball machines please please please make more may I suggest like the Titanic Halloween themed ones more movie related ones just keep them coming please please love love them if you read this and you love pinball you  must buy these no matter what they are freaking amazing good job guys please keep them coming don't stop love your pinball machines highly recommended AAA+++

reviews.review_title: BEST FRIEND EVER FOR 80S OLD GUYS
reviews.review_description:
I LOVE THIS ATMOSPHERE OF THE 80S IS A FLASHBACK VERY ACCURATE AND COOL.
GREAT GREAT WORK ALL DETAILS .
100% AUTHENTICK I GARANTI THAT FEELING.
IM KLASSICK OLD MAN NOSTALGIC IN LOVE WITH THE 80S .
😌👍🏻😉🤘🏻🫵🏻👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻

reviews.review_title: Great game, obnoxious all launch intro
reviews.review_description: To be clear, I am playing in xarcade mode with the arcade2tv-vr rig that has a special mode in pinball fx vr. In this mode, the launch tutorial is not even relevant, as you are not using the oculus controllers at this point at all. It is a glaring oversight that leaves me to wonder what else in this play mode is just half-baked.

Update: I have upgraded my rating to reflect the new update. I’m not sure if this existed before and I never saw it or it was added in the update, but there is a way to turn off scores popping up during gameplay and disabling motion trails on the ball. Turning off these annoying animations has dramatically increased my enjoyment of these tables. To do it, a game must be started and then paused. A screen will pop up and select settings. If I recall it’s the Intable menu and there you can turn off the pop up scoring and motion trail of the ball. If this was part of the update I am very grateful and thank you for listening!

Original review:
First of all I love Pinball FX VR and I have almost all the tables. When I saw the Bethesda pack I bought it immediately without even trying it out because the tables looked so cool and I like almost all the tables in the arcade. This is the first time I’ve been disappointed. I also have Pinball Classic VR. That game has better animations, but it’s goofy and video gamey. I still like it, but I go back to Pinball FX VR because I feel like I’m playing real pinball. This Bethesda pack is a departure from the pinball realism and it belongs in Pinball Classic VR game. Except it’s worse! The developers have made some horrible decisions. They have added score numbers that pop up around the table, which really breaks immersion and reminds the player it’s a video game. They have also added motion trails from the ball, which looks super cheesy and again looks video gamey. The Skyrim game has an annoying dragon that flys right over the table breathing fire on the playfield while gameplay is happening. It also has complicated load outs chosen with flipper combinations. The machines themselves are cool. The Doom machine is especially neat, but I can’t enjoy them because of all the nonsense. Turning off animations doesn’t get rid of the scores either. I am begging the developers to at least remove the score nags and the motion trails.

Still the best pinball vr to date in my opinion. They really need to release some new tables. Its a slow release of new content. Many of us have bought x-arcade pedestals for this game alone and there are so many machines that I have bought on other platforms that need to be released here. All of the Bally/Williams tables are what I really like. Of course I would love to see them get the license for other manufacturers. Hopefully they are not going to abandon this platform.

developer_response.body: Hi! We released Pinball FX VR less than a year ago. It's resource and time-consuming to bring tables to VR, but we will add many more tables in the future! I'm not sure if you've heard, but we've just announced Williams Pinball Volume 10, a Williams three-table pack! Thanks for your kind review. :)

reviews.review_description: I got this game when it launched, and was really disappointed. I refunded it because the resolution was really blurry and the table selection wasn't that great. I just recently bought it to try to play some more pinball, and I found that the issues weren't fixed. The resolution on the tables still looks blurry and pixelated, and, even worse, a lot of the tables suffer from constant frame drops. For pinball, frame drops mean you miss shots, so this definitely ruins a lot of gameplay. The table selection is okay, but they need more tables from FX. They have the classics, but I prefer the Zen Original/IP-based tables. For example, I would love to see the Universal Classics, Jurassic World, Goat Simulator, and Dreamworks tables. They could also use more Zen originals like the Wild West one, the Samurai one, or the camp killer one. They seriously need to work on the resolution and frame drops, and port more tables from the flat screen games.

developer_response.body:
Hi there, thank you for your feedback! Sorry to hear you're experiencing frame rate issues! Could I ask if you were playing in the Favorite slot or at the table's original spot in the room? Also, which game mode were you playing when the frame rate drops occurred? And finally, which headset are you using? Thanks so much.
As for the table selection, we released Pinball FX VR a year ago. It's resource and time-consuming to bring tables to VR, but we will add many more tables in the future!

reviews.review_description:
I have over 8 real pinball machines in my basement and some are even on this app. This app is almost a like for like of the original game play. They really put all the details into this app and even made it repeatable with the awards and trophies you can win. The table verity is great, with some classics, well know ones and even total new virtual ones like fallout.  

I love this game and highly recommend it, I hope they keep coming out with more and can’t wait to see what the have.

developer_response.body: Hi, thank you for your kind review, happy to hear you enjoy the tables! Have a great day!

reviews.review_description: I had a little glitch after an update but the problem is now fixed. I love the variety of tables, the graphics, really everything about this. I also love how responsive the developer was when I encountered a problem. Highly recommend!

developer_response.body:
Hi Glassrose27, thank you for your review! Sorry to hear about your experiences.
Could you please try reinstalling Pinball FX VR? It may help nudge the system in the right direction. We also recommend resetting your headset. Please note that resetting your headset will delete all information stored on the device. However, your progress and other profile-related information will remain unaffected, as they are stored in the cloud. Let us know if your experiences have improved and we will make sure to investigate!

reviews.review_description:
If you really want a pinball machine in your house but can’t because they are too expensive, this is your solution.
I am quite addicted to the Fallout table since it has a bunch of missions.
Sometimes there are some table that will transport you INSIDE the table and everything will look huge
Honestly best VR game for pinball lovers

developer_response.body: Hi IrvingXtrange, thank you for your review! Happy to hear that you like playing Pinball FX VR! We believe that the mixed reality feature brings a whole new level of immersion to this experience. Thank you for your 5 star review!

reviews.review_description: I give it two stars impossible to get real high scores. The ball goes down the middle or sides way to easy

Yes, this is the best pinball game on the meta headsets, but it's fundamentally flawed because they chose to put virtual arms into the game which don't correspond remotely to what your real arms are doing and, a lot of the time, obscure the play field. I've recently seen in their discord that this has been an unresolved issue for months and would caution strongly against buying this as the developers don't seem to care. What a disappointment. For people with a pc I suppose virtual pinball (which is free) is the way to go. 

Also, tables here are not cheap. Yes, they're cheaper than a real pinball but I think it's just predatory to keep putting out more before fixing glaring issues with the game. Typical behaviour from this developer.

Furthermore, the method they have of selling the tables is confusing and can lead to purchasing the same table twice, as some are in bundles and are also available standalone. It's a mess and, again, predatory. Not very "zen" at all, really.

developer_response.body: Hi ChefMaster, thank you for your review! Sorry to hear about your experiences, I have relayed them to the developers who will investigate the situation. We are sorry to hear that you feel this way, I hope that you will give Pinball FX VR another shot in the future.

reviews.review_description:
Game with potential but, big design flaw in the standard controls.No option to remap buttons.. No options for  other controller, beside expensive big arcade controllers from 5 or 7 hundred dollars.. in my opinion a dirty marketing trick to buy a expensive  arcade controller from their partner.
 
Nudging very poor with standard controls.  On a standard controller are buttons enough to play properly. But Zen uses for nudging only on button on purpose . Gesture nudging is not precise and giving stress into wrists. When you change the sensitivity for gestures it is still not close as precise. This kills all the fun. Very disappointed. Could be great game, kind of ruining the experience 



This all is for my the reason to not buy further add ons and go back to flat on my pc.

Answer from developers not useful. Nudging should be done with 2 hands , not with only your thump on your non dominant hand.
Horible trick to let us buy expensive controller.

developer_response.body:
Hi! The product is optimized for use with the official Meta controllers and the Arcade2TV-XR controller, so there is no need to purchase the X-Arcade product if you don't wish to.
Sorry to hear that about nudging! You mentioned you've tried turning down Gesture Nudge Sensitivity. One more thing I can recommend is to calibrate your controller in the system menu:
System Settings → Devices → Controllers → Thumbstick Range and Deadzone → Calibrate Left and Right.
Thank you for the honest review.

reviews.review_title: Wish it offered a volume discount
reviews.review_description:
Between the Oculus Go, my PlayStation 4, and now Quest 2/3, I have spent a ludicrous amount of money on video pinball with this company. 

The three free tables are pretty decent (another review called them ugly, which I think is unfair). The mummy one is my favorite of those.

This version has a ton of QOL upgrades, like a proper arcade to walk around with a sweet 80s vibe (including a cassette player where you can change tapes), mini games and rewards to unlock, a campaign mode, and most impressively an opportunity to demo DLC tables before buying. This is AMAZING. They have a 3-minute/3-ball limit but you can play the demo as many times as you want. This let me rule out the Indiana Jones table that I would have bought (too goofy and retro) but ruled in the Twilight Zone table (was that Golden Earring I heard? <3 <3 <3) I also must have Xena, but I was bitterly disappointed that she wasn't standing next to the table like other characters do.

I can understand the sticker shock, but I spend a lot of time with these tables (they're great to play on a stationary bike) so it's worth it to me.

2026 update: Just bought the Bethesda tables. I own them on Playstation already but the VR tables are slightly different, and of course the VR format brings them alive the way pancake never will. I do agree with criticism about the virtual arms. They randomly jerk up into the air and obscure my view. Please give us a way to disable them.

By far the best pinball games on VR and PC.  Graphics and gameplay are incredible. You can play Games in VR and well as MR. Combined with the X-Arcade stick giving you real buttons and haptics in VR is unbelievable. This game alone is a reason you should own a meta 3 headset. With that being said. I'm giving 4 stars and not a  solid 5 is becuse there is about 5 or 6 gameplay bugs that are well known and reported to them. But refuse to fix them. I don't know why they dont want a 100 percent working product.

developer_response.body: Hi neongambit, thank you for your review! Unfortunately as with any game bugs can arise after release and our goal is to patch them up as best and as soon as we can. Thank you for your positive rating!

reviews.review_title: I HAVE A DREAM BEST PINBALL GAME
reviews.review_description: This is top notch pinball game out there I ever played  i played pinball  at arcade when I was younger  bring me back  memories  words cant  say about this game im humble owen it  highly recommend everyone get  it and you bring the  pinball machines in your room now that incredible  mind blowing  thank you guys making a incredible game number 1 flag ship of game .......

developer_response.body: Hi, thank you so much for the glowing review! We're happy to hear the game brings joy and nostalgia!

reviews.review_description: If you want to play with an external controller, you will have a lot of fun with your virtual arms. Why in hell these stupid arms are nit disable? The developers gives a #&*@ of user complains. Bugfixes, when they will fix it, needs for ever. I will not write more, because i love the game. But zen is a bit incooperative with us users.

developer_response.body: Hi VR-addicted, thank you for your feedback! The suggestion to turn off virtual arms have been noted and relayed to the developers, I hope that in the future we can include it!

reviews.review_description: I've been playing for a few months and have unlocked 90% of all the rewards. Would just really like to see the other Zen pinball tables be able to port into this game and have them all in one game room. The VR Classic tables and the Star Wars tables. Looking forward to playing the new Bethesda tables. Played them on my phone/tablet many times in the past and will be nice to play on a more life-size table simulation. Some gameplay is always lost in the digital format but no way I could afford 50+ real pinball machines for $200. Now ZEN just needs to get them all in one place.

developer_response.body: Hi BCBuzzard50, thank you for your feedback! Your suggestion to try and include previous games in our newest title has been relayed to the developers, unfortunately for some tables there are licensing requirements and other important factors that must be taken into account.

reviews.review_description: Love the game.  Got it and the world cup table when on sale a couple months ago.  Very cool game.  Very realistic, surprisingly so.  I want that arcade flipper thing now.  Will have to get more tables, they are very realistic.

developer_response.body: Hey! Thanks for the 5 Stars! We appreciate it a lot! Have a great day!

reviews.review_description: The immersion and quality is awesome. Just wish we could play with friends in same environment.
This is the best pinball game, plenty of tables and hopefully more to come. My only request is that Fish Tales come to it soon, my favourite ever one.

A magnificent and ultra-realistic game. Everything is polished and customizable: VR mode, MR mode, and also a campaign mode with missions to unlock new rewards, dart mini-games, and interactive gadgets.
The base game offers three original tables (Pinball Noir, Curse of the Mummy, and Sky Pirates: Treasure of the Clouds), but its real appeal lies in the DLC tables.

The DLC catalog is impressive, with new legendary tables regularly released, and this is clearly what keeps the game fresh over time.
I recommend buying your DLC in bundles or on sale to save a bit.
The best part is the compatibility with the Arcade2TV-XR VR controller to simulate playing a real pinball machine. If you love pinball, go for it: you won't be disappointed!

developer_response.body: Hi Majerik, thank you for your feedback! We are glad to hear that you enjoy playing Pinball FX VR! Our developers have worked hard on bringing you a truly immersive and fun experience! Thank you for your recommendation and for your 5 star review!

reviews.review_description: I love it! I think the prices are reasonable. You can't really get pinball machines that cheap. But I would like more shelves to display statues. I hope there are more DLCs like Gozilla or Bethesda coming! ZEN, keep it up!!!

developer_response.body: Hi BakugouK, thanks for your kind words, we appreciate it a lot! A childhood dream come true is just fantastic to hear. :)

reviews.review_description: Anyone have a problem with the game not loading. Started a few months back. Uninstalled and reinstalled still not working.

developer_response.body: Hi there, we're sorry to hear that! I consulted the developers, who suggested that players try waiting around 60-90 seconds when the loading time seems to be extended. After that, the game should load without any issues. If the game's not loading even after that, could you please contact us at support@zenstudios.com, so we can help? Thank you for the review.

reviews.review_title: Ball freezes then u lost it
reviews.review_description: Using a meta 2 the game frezzes up then when it unfreezes u have lost the ball even with all animations turned off still does it ..ive bought this game and nearly all tables the none vr version on both pc and xbox. And soon as this is fixed ill mark it up better. Apart from that it looks amazing and works good untill the lagg freeze ...whens the horor tables comming

developer_response.body: Hi, thanks for your review! Sorry to hear you're experiencing frame rate issues! Could I ask if you were playing in the Favorite slot or at the table's original spot in the room? Also, which game mode were you playing when the frame rate drops occurred? Thanks so much! It would be cool to expand our table lineup with some horror tables. :)

reviews.review_title: Doom's poor performance and other issues
reviews.review_description:
Update :

I tried Doom in MR and the performance is even worse. It's unplayable unless you stay very still. Movement causes the frame rate to tank. This is on Quest 3. How on earth could this have gotten past their QA?

Despite my frustrations with the developers I bought the pack of tables with Doom anyway. In full VR it only stutters when you lean in and it's one of the best, if not THE best pinball tables I've ever played. Spectacular.  I do hope they optimize the new tables, give us an option to get rid of the awful wiggly arms, and fix the teleportation already! How long are they going to leave the UI in this shoddy state?

A Loading Fix:

Luckily the slow loading problem several recent reviews have mentioned seems to be temporary, possibly caused by one time shader caching.  I uninstalled, reinstalled, launched the game, then after the slow load finished I quit the game and rebooted the headset. The next load was much faster. (The reinstall and reboot may not be required, I was just covering all bases.)

Unfortunately once the game loaded nicely I got a nasty surprise. The new Doom table's performance is poor on Quest 3. It looks beautiful but the Quest 3 struggles to render it. It's especially noticeable when you lean in to admire the gorgeous graphics and the frame rate starts hitching. I haven't tried the other new tables yet so I don't know if they're in a similar state. This is especially disappointing since I was going to buy Doom today.

And I find it astonishing that after more than ten months, many updates, and several reviews bringing the broken teleportation to Zen's attention, it's still broken. I wonder if anyone at Zen has even bothered to stand up and test teleportation while turning with their legs. The broken teleportation is disorienting for those of us who use our own legs to turn. The game claims to support room scale and standing play. Until the teleportation is fixed it really doesn't, it's only partial support.

The IK for the arms also glitches when you turn with your own feet and the arms are ridiculously long. It's possible they're at the correct length if you play seated but I don't play pinball sitting down. It would be nice to have an option to switch to floating hands to clean this up.

The arm length and teleportation issues makes me wonder if anyone at Zen ever tested the game from anywhere other than a chair.

developer_response.body: Hi Jioxx, thank you for your review! I have relayed your experiences and suggestions to the developers who will investigate the situation. I hope that we can make the necessary changes in the future and your experiences will improve. Thank you for letting us know and we apologize for the inconvenience.

reviews.review_description: It is very annoying to search for a table I already purchased every time. Please add purchased tables filter

developer_response.body: Hi, thank you for your review. We'll forward your request to the team! Other than that, how are you enjoying Pinball FX VR?

reviews.review_title: Fantastic! But one star until it's fixed
reviews.review_description: This is a fantastic pinball game. I've purchased all the tables. But the blue digital hands that can get right into your face WHILE YOU'RE PLAYING was a ridiculous choice. And there's no way to turn them OFF. One star until this is fixed.

developer_response.body: Hi glarehead, thank you for your review! We have received many suggestions regarding this feature and I hope that we can add it some day! Thank you for letting us know and we apologize for the inconvenience.

reviews.review_description: Love this game

10/10. Honestly, I don't want to do anything else with my time. The physics aren't perfect, but definitely among the best of pinball games I've played. There is the occasional technical issue, a little stutter or freeze, but I trust that's either a hardware thing or it will be addressed in a patch. Now, the big ask. I would pay a premium for a Pinball M VR port, for those tables to be added as a DLC here. What say you, Zen Studios?

developer_response.body: Hi CriticalMatt009, thank you for your review! Happy to hear that you took up the hobby of playing pinball! Regarding your request, I will make sure to let the developers know, I hope that we can keep expanding our table library in the future. Thank you for your 5 star review!

reviews.review_description: The game needs doors or portals that can take you to all the other zen pinball apps. Currently you need to close what your doing to play different tables. Also playing pinball with these controllers is kind of wack. Zen needs to make a controller with buttons. Pinball is supossed to be a tactile game.

developer_response.body: Hi PinballvaultVR, thank you for your review! Sorry to hear about your experiences, if you have purchased a table already you should have uninterrupted access to the table itself without the trial period being active. Could I ask if you have tried reinstalling the game? We might also recommend resetting your headset as well since it could help with your experiences. If the issue persists, please let us know at support@zenstudios.com!

reviews.review_title: Please!add the machine bride of pin bot!
reviews.review_description:
I love this game! The physics are incredible!
Please add the machine bride of pin bot! I grew up on it and would be so nostalgic to be able to play in VR. ￼
￼￼

developer_response.body: Hi, thank you for the fantastic review, glad to hear you like the game.  It would be cool if we could add Bride of Pinbot to Pinball FX VR!

reviews.review_description:
This is a really good game. It’s just like being in the arcade playing a real pinball. 
Would recommend this for any pinball fans out there.

developer_response.body: Hello there! Glad you're having fun, and thank you for the review. :)

reviews.review_description: I have recently downloaded the last 3 pinballs and have had nothing but trouble ever since, I own all the pinballs that have been released and now none of them work, what happens is when I start to play the pause menu keeps popping up it can happen immediately or with in a minute or two I’ve tried resetting all the options back to default , I’ve tried uninstalling and reinstalling pinball fx but nothing has worked. When I play other games or say the Star Wars pinballs there is no problem what’s so ever. Please help because this is now doing my head in, plus the fact I’ve spent a lot of money on pinballs I now can’t use.

developer_response.body: Hi Rassliam, thank you for your feedback! The pausing could be caused by a built in feature for the Meta controllers where if you are holding them completely still for a period of time they will automatically turn off and Pinball FX VR pauses as a consequence. I hope that I could give some clarity to the situation.

reviews.review_title: Best VR Game if you have an X-Arcade.
reviews.review_description:
It’s not cheap to get all these tables and the X-Arcade system, but if you can afford it then it’s the optimal way to plan digital pinball.  I have a stand alone digital pinball game and it is great fun for multiple people, but if I’m playing alone I prefer this game.  The 3D effects make the ramps actual ramps and you can move around to see different angles and the physics are great.  

Also some of the best tables in pinball history.  Just wish they were a little cheaper, but it’s worth it if you can afford it.

developer_response.body: Hi Filazafer, thank you for your feedback! We are glad to hear that you like our latest VR title, thank you for your 5 star rating!

reviews.review_description:
Revising my review. I think they should still put some more info in about how to unlock all the decorations and let you choose which ones to buy using tickets, like a ticket shop, instead of random unlocks. The random decoration unlock is frustrating. Enough that I hated the game.

But if you get beyond that, the experience is amazing. Especially with the xarcade controller.

There's a few other nits I have, but it's good overall and I can't imagine much better for VR pinball.

developer_response.body: Hi HackAfterDark, thank you for your review! We are happy to hear that you enjoy Pinball FX VR, regarding your feedback with the customizations, I have relayed your feedback to the developers who will take a closer look at your suggestion. Thank you for your 5 star review!

reviews.review_description: Enjoying the game, just disappointed it does not have all the same machines that it does on Steam like the Marvel ones.

developer_response.body: Hi, thank you for your review. We released Pinball FX VR a year ago. It's resource and time-consuming to bring tables to VR, but we will add many more tables in the future! Other than that, how do you like the game?

reviews.review_title: An absolute must-have for pinball fans!
reviews.review_description:
I've been playing this every day since release -- a rare thing for a VR game.

Zen's previous offerings, Pinball VR classic and Star Wars Pinball, were both excellent, but with the addition of many RL Williams tables as DLC Pinball FX VR blows them away. Zen has really outdone themselves this time.

The only thing that compares is a real pinball machine -- only I can play this sitting down (or put tables in my room using the mixed reality mode).

And I haven't had time to get bored of the tables; they keep adding more for purchase! 

Up there with Skyrim VR and Beat Saber as one of my all-time favorite VR games!

developer_response.body: Hi SicTim, thank you for your feedback! It's great to hear that you enjoy our game so much! Hopefully we can keep adding more and more tables in the future so will always have something new to enjoy! Thank you for your 5 star rating!

reviews.review_description: This game is incredible, I never thought I would be into pinball. It is probaly one of the best mixed reality experiences on the store. One thing I would like see is the ability to adjust the height of the table in MR mode so you can sit down at play. And it would be awsome if we could vote for the next upcoming tables that our coming out, just a thought.

developer_response.body: Hi Tea1000, thank you for your review! Happy to hear that you like the mixed reality feature! Your requests have been noted and relayed to the developers, we try to take as many suggestions for tables as we can and we hope that we can introduce tables that the community will enjoy playing with! Thank you for your 5 star rating!

reviews.review_description: The machines are great.  What i don't like is having to pay for every machine I like so I can play for more than 3 mins at a time. I bought the app and now I have to pay for every machine?  C'mon that's just bs. There should be at least the ability to choose 4 machines when you buy the app.

developer_response.body: Hi Shagggy, thank you for your review! The base game includes three tables and you have the option to expand your table library with purchasable DLC content. We decided to go with this model because the licensing of some tables had increased costs compared to our original tables.

reviews.review_description:
I love Pinball VR Classic and Star Wars Pinball.

Unfortunately, this version suffers from performance issues on the Quest 3 (even with power saving disabled).

Devs: fix the performance and make this a 5 star game like the others!

Update: tried reinstalling the game and resetting the headset, as advised, but it makes no difference. The game play still stutters on the Quest 3.  Installed it on my Quest 2, and it pays fine on that.

developer_response.body: Hi OrcusMaximus. thank you for your review! Sorry to hear about your experiences, may I ask if you have tried reinstalling the game? It may help with your experiences, we might also recommend resetting your headset. If you are still experiencing issues please let us know at support@zenstudios.com.

reviews.review_description: When I put on my Quest headset FX VR is what I play 95% of the time. The recreations of all the classic Williams pinball tables are incredible. So many amazing titles and surely more on the way. The detail, the precision game play, and the added VR bonuses that can only be had in a VR setting are phenominal.   New users need to understand that the base game comes with 3 tables, created by Zen. They are fine, but the real fun is when you purchase the additional tables from the library. As mentioned , the Williams bundled tables are the best. Attack from Mars, Monster Bash, Black Rose, Medieval Madness, World Cup Soccer and on and on.  If you ever enjoyed pinball, you’ll love this app.  Then the Zen originals are very fun too. Godzilla / King Kong, Doom, Fall Out, Skyrim, Charlie Brown’s Christmas. All great tables with so much game play to be had. To me this is the best game online VR. It’s a must try.

reviews.review_title: UPDATE: Best Pinball game on the store
reviews.review_description:
UPDATE: 
Problem was a language barrier, in my language table guide was translated to tableleader, so i thought its yet another highscore list. 
Now I can enjoy the whole game experience.

.......................................................................
Tables, mechanics, phsyics are great, but, they do not provide any table guides. So u actually can guess only what to do to score big. It is very important for the campaign mode, the mission are really hard and if u dont know what to do.... So therefore im not gonna buy another DLC  which i cant get the campaign missions done, just because i dont know what exactly to do...

developer_response.body: Hi DoNotFollowMe, thank you for your feedback! You can access the table guides for each table by launching a game on the selected table, opening the pause menu and selecting the table guide option where you can find all kinds of important information on any given table. Please let us know if there is anything else we can help you with!

reviews.review_title: Great pinball game with great graphics
reviews.review_description:
This game was the main reason why I got the Quest 3 and it doesn’t disappoint. The game has a great selection of pinball tables with great graphics and effects. The virtual arcade is beautiful but the MR is also really nice.

I also bought the Arcade2TV-XR controller which adds an additional layer of realism. The controller is very well integrated and gives the sensation of standing behind a physical table. 

Highly recommended game!

developer_response.body: Hi CoralSquid4771, thank you for your review! We are happy to hear about your experiences, the controller brings a whole new level of immersion to Pinball FX VR which is the main reason for our support! Thank you for your 5 star rating!

reviews.review_description: Hi, I have a quest 2 and have bought nearly all the table add-ons, but now my set keeps glitching, and have notifications the there is not enough memory to run this game now - is there a solution to this, do i have to uninstall certain tables and if so, how do i do this, but overall a must have for pinball lovers

developer_response.body: Hi tthumpa88, thank you for your feedback! We might recommend resetting your headset and downloading each game on your headset one by one to see about your performance and to get a clearer picture of your memory issue. Please let us know at support@zenstudios.com if you are still experiencing any issues!

reviews.review_description: I have probably 40+ hours in this game and won’t be stopping anytime soon.  The tables are a top notch, I love all the different modes you can play on them. The 3 achievements for each table, unlocks, leaderboards to chase and amount of customization is awesome. I hope they continue to add tables like they have been. I’ll be playing them all 🙂

developer_response.body: Hi Tcollich, thank you for your feedback! We are glad to hear that you like our latest VR project, we hope that we can keep introducing new tables to the platform! Thank you for your 5 star rating!

reviews.review_title: No Table Haptics??? R U Kidding me???
reviews.review_description:
Meh!
Seasoned veteran of various VR pinball games (FX2 VR, VPX VR, Future Pinball (BAM) VR, etc.), many of which are free.
Why are the haptics only felt while moving around the VR rooms and environments and not while standing at the actual tables and using the flippers????
This is on day one, so perhaps it's a glitch, but...wow...is this the reality of how the game plays after your extensive testing???
Not really interested in purchasing the overpriced DLC if this is the reality.

UPDATE : OK, so haptics were added for the Quest motion controllers...VERY much appreciated, thank you!
For us pincab players, can you PLEASE add standard XBox gamepad controller options like your other games have. I know that there are keyboard command options, but WOW, they are all over the place for actually being able to play the game.
Also, is DOFLinx an option, or perhaps if this ever comes to Steam?
Cheers!

developer_response.body: Hi Kranky_Pantz, thank you for your updated review! I have relayed your suggestion to the developers who will take a closer look at your request. Thank you for letting us know!

reviews.review_description:
If you want to play pinball in VR this is a 5 star game and you should get it.  The DM is a little pricey, but not unreasonably so considering the high quality licensed tables.  In my youth I would pour hundreds of quarters into these games (each worth 75 cents today due to inflation), so $10 for a table does not seem ridiculous to me.  The pinball games rock and I love that they have guides explaining how to play them.

This gets only 3 stars because the navigation is so clunky.  The developers do release updates with fixes for the tables, but simple navigation bugs like the fact that when you teleport you often are not facing the right direction has never been fixed.  The audio when you are wandering around the arcade pans weird.  The menus and pop-up notifications are distracting or annoying.  You can’t just restart a game without having navigate through some menu.  I have X-Arcade which is so fun but they still make you use the controllers to navigate around the arcade and the menus in between games.  The option menu has a lot of neat options but it’s too easy to click on the wrong thing by accident.    

Playing the game in AR mode is fun and avoids some of these issues though you are limited by your space how many tables you can fit in your room. 

I would actually prefer just a simple fast menu that lets me scroll and pick the game I want to play.  The “map” feature on the wrist menu sort of does this but not very well because the controls are so fiddly, you are as likely to accidentally switch to AR mode than to successfully use it for navigation.

The animations are kind of fun for a game or two.  You might like them, but I find them distracting and turned most of them off.

The prizes are not that awesome, just skins.

Great pinball, but the rest of the game needs work before I can give it a better rating.

developer_response.body: Hi ybrianc, thank you for your review! Your suggestions and experiences have been forwarded to the developers, most of the issues mentioned have been stylistic choices to compliment the immersion but we understand that some of them may be inconvenient. Thank you for letting us know!

reviews.review_description: Game won't load. Waited 5 minutes, ridiculous. Tried uninstalling , still didn't work. Please fix, worked fine before the update.

developer_response.body: Hi tjs.71390, thank you for your review! Sorry to hear about your experiences, I have relayed them to the developers and I hope that in the future your experiences will improve.

reviews.review_title: Good physics and passthrough is awesome.
reviews.review_description:
Always wanted to own a pinball machine in real life. This is way cheaper and takes up less space and really does feel immersive.

Pretty smooth gameplay. Ive found playing in mixed reality and turning off the extra animation effects gets the most buttery fps.

Great physics. Though the tables seem slightly more forgiving than real life. Though I did discover a 'pro' mode which makes it more steep and realistic.

Paired with the arcade2tv-xr table with haptics kit and this is really freaking sweet!

developer_response.body: Hi Parsonsproject, thank you for your review! Happy to hear that you enjoy Pinball FX VR! The Arcade-XR controller can really bring a whole new level of immersion to our game, thank you for your 5 star rating!

reviews.review_description: I don't like how advertisement for some flipper controller was placed in the arcade. It makes you feel like it's not really your place. I wouldn't freely hang up advertisement for something I don't want in my actual place. It feels forced.

developer_response.body: Hi SpicyAftermath, thank you for your feedback! Sorry to hear about your experiences, I have relayed them to our developers who will take a closer look at your suggestion.

reviews.review_description: This is the easiest way to play pinball in VR and has lots of tables plus more coming. It also gets regular updates and bug fixes. I also love the collab with X-Arcade for the controller making it highly immersive. I think it needs the simultaneous touch controllers and hand-tracking mode so you can have the controllers sync the height for the x-arcade stick and hand tracking for your hands and menu interaction while using the x-arcade stick. adding hand tracking in this mode would be great. would also like multiplayer lobbies where you can play with friends

developer_response.body: Hi lbeavisc, tahnk you for your review! It's great to hear you enjoy playing Pinball FX VR with the X-Arcade controller! Your request have been relayed to the developers, I hope that we can implement features like this to make the experience more comfortable and immersive! Thank you for your 5 star review!

reviews.review_description: Although I greatly enjoy Pinball VR Classic, Pinball FX VR is the very best VR pinball game!  It is the most immersive pinball with the greatest special effects, sound effects, and games.  It has many tables and bundles of various genres to enjoy throughout its huge pinball hall.  I love the new Skyrim, Doom, and Fallout bundle which are amazing, although I really like Star Trek so The Next Generation table is one of my favorites too.  If you want to get just one pinball game, this is the one to get!

developer_response.body: Hello! Thank you very much for your support and 5-star review!

reviews.review_description:
Bride of Pinbot! PLEASE :)

This is, without question, one of the standout games for VR! I was so impressed, I'm repurchasing all the tables over even though I already own them on Steam. (Sure it would be nice to not have to but this is so impressive I don't mind!)

I would love, if legal issues do not prevent, to have the Star Wars Tables be able to be ported over. I kinda does not make sense to have to leave the arcade just to play those tables. I know that app came first but now that we have an arcade, it would be nice to include those.

I know you can't just add the missing tables that you have already done for Steam as you need to add some VR effects to each but I hope you continue to port them over.

Every "real" pinball lover dreams of having their own Pinball Arcade and this is the only way for us to do so unless we are very wealthy and can rent a building and buy all the real machines :)

So a HUGE thank you for this outstanding game but please fill in the missing games ASAP... we are hungry for more!

developer_response.body: Hi jrb531, thank you for your review! We are so glad to hear that you like playing with Pinball FX VR! We hope that we can keep adding tables to our growing table library in the future, your request to also include the Star Wars tables has been noted and relayed to the developers. Thank you for your 5 star review!

reviews.review_title: Fun pinball and a few requests
reviews.review_description:
I absolutely love pinball and play it almost every day.
It’s also really fun how the basement keeps expanding every time a new table is added.I’m playing on Quest 3, and I have a couple of requests.
The frame rate drops extremely low on the table that’s straight ahead when you go down the stairs from the lobby (the spot where Sky Pirates is placed by default).
Both Mummy and Noir also start stuttering when moved to that same location.
The only current workaround seems to be setting the front table as a “Favorite” and playing it that way.
Even using Quest Games Optimizer doesn’t help much.Please fix this issue.Another request: I’d love more places to hang posters.
It’s a shame that even though we can win posters from the roulette, there are very few spots to actually put them up.
There seems to be plenty of space, so at the very least, it would be great if we could display as many posters as there are tables.Looking forward to more new tables and updates!

developer_response.body: Hi Sunshower, thank you for your feedback! I have relayed your experiences and suggestions to the developers, I hope we can keep making positive changes in the future. Thank you for letting us know and we apologize for the inconvenience.

reviews.review_description: I tried the demo and there were some time limits so I assumed when I bought it for 10 bucks, the restrictions would be gone. But, no you don’t really own these games in here what the $10 gets you is a chance to buy them and they cost multiple times $10 or you can just play them with limit so that if you’re three minutes into a game it just cuts you off.  This is a new low and I’m really surprised that Meta allowed it to happen. You would think they would do a better job of protecting their customers.

developer_response.body: Hi CostaWalker, thank you for your review! The base game includes 3 of our VR pinball tables as advertised and players can also purchase DLC content with more tables if they prefer. You also have the chance to try them out in our trial mode so you could make a more careful decision if you would like to buy more of our tables. Thank you for letting us know!
